title: SDLC harness config
description: Project policy the SDLC engines read from planning/harness.json. The harness (engine code) carries the mechanism; this file carries the project facts (validation commands, whether a UI-test stage exists, ports/routes). If the file is absent, the engines fall back to the spec's `## Validation Commands` section and disable the UI-test stage — they ship no stack defaults. MVP shape: validation.checks[] + uiTest.enabled (and its enabled-only fields).

$schema.description: Editor hint pointing at this schema (e.g. ../.claude/workflows/harness.schema.json).

stack.description: Informational label only (e.g. 'rust', 'python-fastapi', 'nextjs'). Not consumed by engine logic.

_comment.description: Free-form note for humans editing the file. Ignored by the engines.

validation.description: The always-run validation suite the Test stage executes, in order.

checks.description: Ordered checks run top-to-bottom in the Test stage.

uiTest.description: Optional UI smoke-test stage. enabled:false makes the whole stage a no-op (verdict SKIPPED). The remaining fields are read only when enabled.

enabled.description: Whether the pipeline runs a UI smoke check. Non-web projects set false.

devServerCommand.description: Command that starts the dev server (e.g. 'npm run dev').

readySignal.description: Substring in the dev-server output that signals readiness (e.g. 'Ready in').

port.description: Base port the dev server listens on. In parallel task runs the engine uses port + taskNumber (hardcoded behavior).

routes.description: Routes to smoke-check once the server is ready (e.g. ['/', '/about']).

purpose.description: One-line description of what the check gates. For the authoritative test command, say so here.

gates.description: Whether this check's failure blocks the review verdict. Replaces the old hardcoded 'jest is authoritative' rule.
